sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: This matchup pits Atlético Madrid, currently sitting in 3rd place in the league with 67 points, against Real Sociedad, who are positioned 12th with 43 points. The game is set to take place at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano in Madrid, promising a thrilling encounter under the whistle of referee José María Sánchez Martínez, known for averaging 5.6 yellow cards per game. Atlético Madrid will be looking to leverage their strong home advantage, while Real Sociedad aim to climb the table with an upset.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Atlético Madrid has dominated with 3 home wins, while 2 games ended in draws, and Real Sociedad has yet to secure a victory. Their most recent encounter on October 6, 2024, resulted in a 1-1 draw, with Real Sociedad’s Takefusa Kubo earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.3 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends also show Atlético Madrid with no losses in their last 7 meetings against Real Sociedad, often taking the lead early as they’ve been the first to score in 6 of the last 7 games.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Key Stats
sections.text: Atlético Madrid boasts an impressive defensive record, conceding only 27 goals this season, ranking 2nd in the league for the least goals conceded. They’ve also scored 56 goals, placing them 4th in total goals scored, with a notable 75% of their goals assisted, ranking 2nd in the league. Real Sociedad, on the other hand, has struggled recently, with a streak of 4 games without a win, and they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches against Atlético. Additionally, both teams have scored in 5 of their last 7 head-to-head encounters, suggesting a high likelihood of goals on both ends.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players to Watch
sections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Antoine Griezmann stands out with a 7.21 average Sofascore rating, 8 goals, and 6 assists this season, while also shining against Real Sociedad with an average rating of 7.48, 2 goals, and 2 assists in the last 5 matchups. Another key player is Axel Witsel, ranking 2nd in the league for percentage of ground duels won at 73.9%. On the Real Sociedad side, Sergio Gómez is a creative force with 14 big chances created, ranking 5th in the league, alongside 2 goals and 5 assists. Real Sociedad’s Jon Martin also impresses, leading the league with an 81.7% aerial duels won rate, while Nayef Aguerd ranks 4th with 72% ground duels won.

sections.subtitle: Emerging and Supporting Talent
sections.text: Beyond the top performers, Atlético Madrid’s Alexander Sørloth has overperformed his expected goals with 13 goals from 11.95 xG, providing a lethal edge up front. Midfielder Rodrigo de Paul also contributes with 3 goals and 4 assists, maintaining a solid 7.05 average rating. For Real Sociedad, Mikel Oyarzabal offers consistent attacking output with 8 goals and 2 assists, while Martín Zubimendi anchors the midfield with a 7.08 average rating. These players could play pivotal roles in deciding the outcome of the match.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Game Expectations
sections.text: Atlético Madrid’s strength lies in their late-game scoring, with 20 goals (25.9% of their total) coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, indicating a tendency to push hard in the closing stages. Real Sociedad shows a more balanced scoring distribution, with 11 goals in the 76th-90th minute period, but struggles defensively late on as evidenced by their lack of clean sheets recently. Atlético’s 52% average ball possession (7th in the league) and high pressing (4th in successful final third passes per game) could dominate Real Sociedad, who will need to rely on counter-attacks and set pieces to exploit any defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Referee Impact and Match Discipline
sections.text: With referee José María Sánchez Martínez officiating, expect a tightly controlled game as he averages 5.6 yellow cards and 0.25 red cards per match. Atlético Madrid commits a high number of fouls, ranking 18th with 371 fouls this season, though they’ve managed to keep yellow cards relatively low at 72 (6th fewest in the league). Real Sociedad will need to be cautious, as their defensive struggles could lead to tactical fouls under pressure from Atlético’s attacking prowess, potentially pushing the card count higher.
